title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Landmark Heading Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 7848 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.

Inspect the Landmark Heading Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Landmark Heading Model.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-1hjejj" that uses case 9789 to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Accessibility Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-1hjejj
# Competency: ssg-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 9789: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_accessibility_evidence_1hjejj(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1hjejj = "1hjejj-jjejh1-9789"
    assert evidence_variant_1hjejj
    evidence_axes_1hjejj = (
        "reader1hjejj",
        "source9789",
        "parsejjejh1",
        "route1hjejj9789",
        "artifact97891hjejj",
        "recoveryjjejh19789",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1hjejj)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"
